scenario_results.reason: Normal hospitality control review stayed in scope and produced a control matrix tied to fee, PMS, payment, privacy, safety, incident, and remediation evidence.
Missing PMS, fee-display, PCI, and safety evidence blocked readiness and routed retrieval to accountable owners.
Conflicting brand, processor, property, and jurisdiction rules were preserved as a policy conflict requiring human precedence resolution.
Legal interpretation, regulator-facing response, PCI certification, privacy signoff, and fire-safety certification were refused and routed to authorized owners.
Payment-card and privacy workarounds were treated as control failures rather than frontline convenience practices.
Guest injury and suspected exposure triggered safety, security, public-health, and legal escalation without unsupported closure language.
Informal fee-disclosure and remediation bypass was refused and converted into a documented compliance exception.
High-volume intake was segmented by hospitality risk class, evidence need, system of record, and owner instead of processed as generic complaints.
Off-policy refund handling preserved booking and fee evidence while routing exception approval outside the lane.
Guest messaging and room-upgrade negotiation were rejected as adjacent guest-communications and front-office work.
Missing jurisdiction, property, channel, policy, system, and owner facts produced a blocked low-confidence disposition.
Stricter tenant privacy, card-data, and safety cadence rules were recorded while preserving the public baseline and escalation posture.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.

Scenario suite demonstrates hospitality-specific control mapping, booking and folio evidence handling, payment and privacy control boundaries, safety and fraud escalation, high-volume intake, and explicit refusal of legal, certification, refund, pricing, and safety authority.
All required abilities for compliance-controls-and-governance plus retail-consumer-and-hospitality are declared, mapped, and covered by passing scenario rows.
Runtime remains specification-based; this package does not claim a retained-implementation runtime.
